description: Adversarial-survival prediction — would the claims hold up against §103 obviousness, §102 anticipation, IPR / EPO opposition / district court validity challenge? Inherently a Track A signal: requires comparison to ground-truth historical patents that survived adversarial review.
sub_criteria: Predicted survival probability based on Track A ground-truth comparison
Juristat API — claim-level prosecution outcome prediction
Lex Machina — litigation outcome priors for analogous claims

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Claims align with ground-truth survived claims for the same disclosure; predicted IPR survival > 80%; analogous-claim litigation outcomes favorable.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Strong alignment with ground truth; minor scope deltas would not change adversarial outcome.

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Partial overlap with ground truth; predicted survival 50-80%; litigation outcome priors mixed.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Material divergence from survived claims; predicted survival 20-50%; analogous claims often invalidated.

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Claims would be readily invalidated; predicted survival < 20%; no defensible position against motivated adversary.

track_a_signals: Independent-claim scope overlap (intersection-over-union) with survived claims
Juristat survival prediction score for the candidate's independent claim
track_b_protocol: Track B contributes minimally to Dim 3 — reviewers only flag claims that, in their professional judgment, would not survive an IPR they have personally seen. Default Track B score for Dim 3 is the median across reviewers; outliers (≥2-point deviation) trigger discussion before merge.
